items.title: Addictive drug abuse and depression-a focus on epigenetics
items.author: Feng Guo
items.content: This Review article explores shared epigenetic mechanisms linking addictive drug use and depression, highlighting DNA methylation, histone modifications and non-coding RNAs as key contributors to their comorbidity.

items.title: Early life adversity impairs visually evoked innate defensive behaviors via oxytocin signaling
items.author: Liping Wang
items.content: ELA induced by social deprivation on postnatal days 10–20 impairs looming-evoked innate defensive behaviors via decreased oxytocin receptor mRNA levels in the intermediate and deep layers of the superior colliculus.

items.title: LICHEN enables light-chain immunoglobulin sequence generation conditioned on the heavy chain and experimental needs
items.author: Charlotte M. Deane
items.content: LICHEN is a computational approach for generating antibody light sequences conditioned on a heavy chain, with optional germline and antigen-binding constraints. In vitro validation confirms LICHEN’s value across antibody engineering scenarios.

items.title: Mapping the marine distribution of eulachon (Thaleichthys pacificus) in the Northeast Pacific using environmental DNA
items.author: Ryan P. Kelly
items.content: This manuscript combines eDNA data with a statistical model to describe the spatial distribution of eulachon, a threatened smelt. DNA was positively associated with river mouths, offshore banks, warmer waters, and areas with high prey concentration.

items.title: Dendritic heterosynaptic plasticity arises from calcium-based input learning
items.author: Christian Tetzlaff
items.content: Our computational model shows that calcium diffusion between spines via dendrites drives heterosynaptic plasticity, providing new learning mechanisms based on timing and spine location.

items.title: Spatial multiomics reveals irreversible electroporation-induced immuno-metabolic characteristics of the inflammatory margin in liver cancer
items.author: Tianan Jiang
items.content: Multiomics reveal an irreversible electroporation-specific immunosuppressive microenvironment within the inflammatory margin, maintained by lipid metabolic reprogramming, suggesting combinatory therapeutic targets.

items.title: A signature-agnostic test for differences between tumor mutation spectra reveals carcinogen and ancestry effects
items.author: Kelley Harris
items.content: A statistical test identifies significant differences in mutational biases between groups of tumors, revealing carcinogen- and ancestry-associated effects that are often missed by standard mutational signature analysis approaches.

items.title: Achieving more human brain-like vision via human EEG representational alignment
items.author: Julie D. Golomb
items.content: EEG-aligned fine-tuning makes artificial vision models more brain-like, enhancing model-human representational similarity across EEG, fMRI, and behavior.
